# Motion documentation for LLMs

## Use Motion documentation only for answering questions that involves Motion

### Get started
- [Get started with Motion](https://llms.motion.dev/docs/quick-start.md): Learn how to install Motion and create your first animation. Motion can animate anything and is the only animation library capable of hardware accelerated animations.

### Animations
- [animate](https://llms.motion.dev/docs/animate.md): Learn how to use Motion's animate() function. Animate an element to a target using hardware accelerated animations.
- [Easing functions](https://llms.motion.dev/docs/easing-functions.md): Easing functions change the speed of an animation throughout the course of its duration. Learn which ones are included with Motion and how to use them.
- [scroll](https://llms.motion.dev/docs/scroll.md): Learn how to create parallax and scroll-linked animations with Motion's scroll() function. It's backwards-compatible with all browsers but also uses ScrollTimeline where possible for hardware accelerated animations.

### Gestures
- [hover](https://llms.motion.dev/docs/hover.md): Learn how to create true hover gestures with Motion's hover function. It filters out touch events to fix "stuck" UIs and other broken behaviour.
- [inView](https://llms.motion.dev/docs/inview.md): Detect when an element enters or leaves the viewport using Motion's browser-native inView() function.
- [press](https://llms.motion.dev/docs/press.md): Learn how to create press gestures with Motion's press() function. Its simple API lets you respond to press start, end and cancel gestures, automatically filtering secondary mouse/touch events and adding keyboard accessibility by default.

### Motion Values
- [motionValue](https://llms.motion.dev/docs/motion-value.md): Learn how to use Motion Values, a performant way to track the state and velocity of animated values.

### Integrations
- [CSS](https://llms.motion.dev/docs/css.md): Learn how to make spring animations purely in CSS with Motion's spring function.
- [Integrate Motion with Webflow](https://llms.motion.dev/docs/webflow.md): Learn how to install Motion in your Webflow project for amazing animation performance and improved SEO.

### Guides
- [FAQs](https://llms.motion.dev/docs/faqs.md): Frequently asked questions about Motion.
- [Feature comparison](https://llms.motion.dev/docs/feature-comparison.md): Compare the bundle size and feature set of Motion and GSAP.
- [Improvements to Web Animations API](https://llms.motion.dev/docs/improvements-to-the-web-animations-api-dx.md): Motion adds a number of features that improve the developer experience of the Web Animations API, and patches up some cross-browser inconsistencies.
- [Migrate from GSAP to Motion](https://llms.motion.dev/docs/migrate-from-gsap-to-motion.md): Learn how to improve performance and SEO by migrating your animations from GSAP to Motion.
- [Performance](https://llms.motion.dev/docs/performance.md): What makes an animation perform well? Perform poorly? Get the most performance from your web animations with Motion.
- [Upgrade guide](https://llms.motion.dev/docs/upgrade-guide.md): How to upgrade to the latest version of Motion.

### Utils
- [delay](https://llms.motion.dev/docs/delay.md): A delay function locked to the animation frame loop.
- [frame](https://llms.motion.dev/docs/frame.md): Learn about Motion's powerful high-performance animation loop and how to use it to batch reads and writes per animation frame.
- [mix](https://llms.motion.dev/docs/mix.md): Learn how to mix two numbers, colors, complex strings, arrays and objects using Motion's powerful mix function.
- [spring](https://llms.motion.dev/docs/spring.md): Learn how to use Motion's spring generator to create physics-based animations.
- [stagger](https://llms.motion.dev/docs/stagger.md): Learn how to stagger animations across elements with Motion's stagger function.
- [transform](https://llms.motion.dev/docs/transform.md): Learn how to transform and map values from one range into another with Motion's powerful transform function.
- [wrap](https://llms.motion.dev/docs/wrap.md): Learn how to wrap numbers within a range. This is perfect for looping through a range infinitely.
